desc:
You find yourself on a shady path in the forest. The path is well-prepared and
makes travel here easy. The forest is bordered to the north by hills and rocks,
and you think you hear the sound of water running among the trees. The main path
seems to continue east and west, and you can enter deeper into the forest to the
south.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
You find yourself at the fringes of a forest. There are few trees here, but you
notice the forest grows much thicker to the east and south of you. The path here
is easy to walk upon; it is probably used a lot. The trees are green, and you
feel relaxed walking here. The path continues to the west and south. To the
north you can see mostly mountainous terrain.
name: Bend in the Forest Trail

desc:
The trees are green, most of them tall pines, and the scent makes you feel like
staying here for a while. The brushwood between the trees has been removed and
set up at several places. You notice a small path in the bushes to the east,
while you can stay on the main path by going north or south.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
Walking along this well-travelled path makes you wonder where it might lead. You
can hear the sound of water deeper into the forest. The trees grow denser to the
west. You can stay on the path to the north or you can enter the forest to the
west. To the east the forest clears out.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
You follow this pleasant trail leading east-west. On both sides of this path,
you can see some flower patches and low trees. The path continues into a forest
to the west, while to the north you can spot a few hilltops.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You are walking on a well-prepared trail, making it easy to travel upon. The
sweet scent of flowers makes you feel comfortable. On both sides of you there is
a flower patch. The main trail continues to the west and north, but you can spot
a small path to the south of you.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path slowly makes its way down. Walking here is not as easy as you thought
it should be. There are huge sharp rocks lying here, making it dangerous if you
plan to move fast. To the east you can see the main trail, while to the north
you can enter some rough terrain. The trail continues to the south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You can barely follow the very tiny marks of a trail here. All around you, there
are patches of flowers and high grass covers your feet. Still you feel pretty
lonely out here. The path continues east and west.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You find yourself standing on a narrow path. The ground here looks rather stony,
but still you can see small patches of grass and flowers. There are no trees
growing around here, only small bushes. The path continues to the north and
west.
name: Worn Trail by the River Lhun

desc:
You find yourself standing on a windy path. The ground here looks rather stony,
but still you can see small patches of grass and flowers. The vegetation is
sparse, only a few crooked low trees grow here. The wind is chilling here,
making you feel a bit uncomfortable. The path continues to the east and south.
name: Worn Trail by the River Lhun

desc:
You find yourself standing on a windy path. The ground here looks rather stony,
but still you can see small patches of grass and flowers. The vegetation is
sparse, only a few crooked low trees grow here. The landscape around you slowly
changes here. To the north you can see a big clearing, while the path continues
to the west. To the east you can cross the river by a big, wooden bridge.
name: Worn Trail by the River Lhun

desc:
This bridge was built with strong wooden planks. Your steps are solid, since the
construction seems able to hold your weight. Below the bridge a river of slow
flowing waters can be seen; with some caution you should be able to put a boat
on it, and sail. To the west and the east you see two paths, conveniently
connected by the bridge.
name: A Wooden Bridge

desc:
The path becomes more overgrown here with weeds and flowers. It still seems to
be used, but it needs much work to be really comfortable to travel upon. The
path seem to lead further east up towards the mountains you can see with its
snowy peaks. You notice the path is blocked by a huge rock, preventing further
exploration to the east. To the west you can see a wooden bridge.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path is still pretty good to walk upon. It still seems to be frequently
used, but it looks like it will be harder to travel as you continue more to the
north. Big trees seem to surround the path here, and you can see a few of them
standing close to the river bank, with their heavy branches leaning out in the
water. The path continues to the north and south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path is still pretty good to walk upon. It still seems to be frequently
used, but it looks like it will be harder to travel as you continue more to the
north. The vegetation is thicker here, and you see some tall trees around you,
probably pines. The river is lazily floating southwards at your northwest side,
while the path continues to the north and west.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You are walking on a broad path, with the relaxing sound of a river to the north
and west of you. The water looks clear, but you would need a boat to cross it.
You see some small bushes here, and also a lot of flowers are growing here. The
path continues to the east and south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path you follow is easy to follow. You wish all paths should be so easy to
follow indeed. On all sides of the path you can see some thick grass growing.
The grass is tall and you imagine you see small things moving around in it.
Here, the flowers grow in abundance. The air is filled with the sweet scent of
flowers, and you feel happy walking here. The path continues to the north and
south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail keeps advancing further into lands unknown to the common man. Just
those with the spirit of adventure, and a firm grasp on their weapons, dare to
walk where no one could hear their screams. But this part of Arda seems quite
peaceful, and you feel safe. This trail seems to be every step less defined, and
you realise few come around here. It goes north and west.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail seems to continue forever. You can hear the sound of water not far
away here. The trail seems to be well prepared and it makes you happy to find
such good trails still in Arda. You can spot small flowers at the side of the
road, and you feel relaxed.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Again the trail curves sharply to the west and north. To the east you see a flat
endless area. From the north you seem to hear the river. Nature seems pure
around here. Nevertheless, small traces of civilization could be found here as
in any other place in Arda.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The trail advances through the dense woods, whose trees form impressive walls
along its sides. The muffled sounds of the river seem to come from the north and
west, but you are totally unable to pass through the wilderness to reach it from
here. You'd better use the road that goes south and east.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
Again the trail makes another curve, now to the west and the north. To the east
and south you can see a small but impossible to climb cliff. There seems to be
something there on the rock, but you cannot guess what could it be. Somewhere,
further into the mountains, stands the Dwarven Home.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The air here is clean and fresh. Nature holds sway over all the land, apparently
untouched by mortal hand, except for this worn trail upon which you find
yourself. The trail bends from the south to the east here, following the line of
the river. Bushes and trees stand at intervals between it and you, but do not
block out the view of the gently flowing water. To the north the river can be
reached from the bank, while to the west you can see a small island. Soft silt
and shallow water make it impossible to reach from here though.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The lands both to the east, and to the west of the trail boast a natural, rugged
beauty. The sound of grasshoppers can be heard from the grass nearby, and from
somewhere to the west the croaking of frogs seems to originate. This is usually
a peaceful spot, although the local wildlife are regularly disturbed by
travellers. The river lies to the west, but to the east a sandstone rock cliff
prevents movement in that direction, although it gets lower to the south where
it eventually disappears.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail curves sharply to the west and north. East of you, you see a flat
area. Sometimes you see a stag run by and a deer here and there. The plains seem
to be filled with life and vegetation. It looks as if mother nature blessed this
place.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
This worn and frequented trail bends here to south and east. The soothing
whisper of the river reaches you from the west, where you could use a canoe to
navigate the calm waters. Around you, the land remains quiet, as if it were
expecting something. It will probably be that way forever.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The trail continues on through the virgin lands. To the north the trail sinks
deeply into vast plains and dense forests. To the south you see it bend. There
is a quiet and neat field to the east. Grasshoppers keep on playing and hopping
between the flowers.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail bends here and you are compelled to go either north or west here. The
river flows to the west. To the east you can see a small forest. What forest
creatures could live there?
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
Walking north you leave the bridge behind you, still amazed by the mastery of
the workers who built it. You wonder about the strength and iron health of those
who lifted the heavy iron beams over the stream. The mighty river to your west
and north looks blue and clear. You might bathe or you might follow the trail
southwards or eastwards.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The path is hard to keep track of and after a little while you can get quite
frustrated. The path seems to continue to the west into the hills. To the south
some small hilltops appear.
name: Rocky Trail

desc:
The path you are trying to follow is extremely difficult to walk upon because of
all the rocks and boulders here constantly obstructing your way all the time.
The wind blows with a cold chill here. To your excitement you can see a tiny
trail to the west and to the south you can see small hills rise.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The path you try to keep travel along, is extremely difficult to walk upon
because of all the rocks and boulders here constantly obstructing your way all
the time. To the north, a large gorge is blocking your way, making travel there
impossible, while to the east you are happy to see an old house. Both to the
west and south, the landscape is the same as here, barren, cold and hard to walk
upon.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
You are standing in front of an old inn, probably not used for several decades.
Judging by the look of the house, it looks like it was abandoned a long time
ago. The roof, or what's left of it looks really rotten and the nonexisting door
and glasses in the window adds up the general look of an really old building.
There's a little fireplace here, and you can see a few bushes growing nearby.
name: In Front of an Old Lodge

desc:
The dry cold wind lashing over the cold plains up here makes travelling here a
bit uncomfortable. The grass covers your feet entirely and you can't see any
special path to follow. To the west you can see an old house, while the plains
continues to the north.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
When you stand in the middle of this plain, you feel like being lost in the
wilderness up here. The grass covers your feet entirely and you can't see any
special path to follow. To your puzzlement you can see an old building to the
southwest of you. What kind of people live up here in these desolate areas? More
plains open up to the south and east.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
When you stand in the middle of this plain, you feel like being lost in the
wilderness up here. The grass covers your feet entirely and you can't see any
special path to follow. You can see plains in all your directions.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
When you stand in the middle of this plain, you feel like being lost in the
wilderness up here. The grass covers your feet entirely and you can't see any
special path to follow. To the south you can see a hilltop, somewhat bigger than
all the other small hills to the south.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
When you stand in the middle of this plain, you feel like being lost in the
wilderness up here. The grass covers your feet entirely and you can't see any
special path to follow. The wind blows gently in your face making you feel
somewhat refreshed. You can see more plains to the west and northwest, but you
can also notice a little trail to the north and east of you.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
You are walking on a small trail leading towards more plains. The wind blows
more cold up here, making you move on faster towards your unknown destination.
The trail is well-kept and you start to wonder who uses it that regulary up here
in this cold area of Arda. Here, the trail seems however to end and you can't
find any special track to follow. To the south and west of you there are plains,
but the path continues to the east.
name: A Small Path

desc:
You are walking on a small trail leading towards more plains. The wind blows
more cold up here, making you move on faster towards your unknown destination.
The trail is well-kept and you start to wonder who uses it that regularly up
here in this cold area of Arda. The trail continues west and south of you.
name: A Small Path

desc:
There are not that many bushes and grass left around you, and the surrounding
has become a bit more rocky. To the far north you can see big mountains rise up
in the distance, and it make you feel small and unimportant. Still - you can
catch the sight of some grassy fields to the west, but the path continues to the
north.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The path seems to be little used and it's not easy to keep track of it all the
time. On both sides of the trail, you can see grass growing and you can hear
some birds singing around you. This must surely be an uninhabited land. You feel
lonely up here and the endless plains and rocky terrain adds that up much.
name: A Tiny Trail

desc:
It must have been a long time since this inn was populated and gave shelter to
weary travellers. Some old rotten furniture, shelves, a cot and a broken stone
fireplace are all you can spot at first sight. The floor is littered with dirt
and dead leaves. There is a ventilation shaft over the fireplace, but of no need
as half of the roof is missing. The only exit is back south.
name: Inside the Old Lodge

desc:
The stairway gives out creaking noises when you step on it, still it seems to
hold your weight. A little backroom opens up to you when your eyes get used to
the darkness down here. The room is very narrow, and the loose planks on the
walls make this a very dull place. It must have been used as a wine and maybe
beer storage room when the inn was operative. Still you wonder who made all the
footprints you can clearly see on the floor. On the eastern wall you can see
huge beerbarrels.
name: Old Cellar

desc:
You have made your way into the secret hideout of the local robbers of the Blue
Mountains. It must be here that they stay when they are not sneaking around the
trails, robbing innocent travellers on their way. On the southern side of the
room are some beds and you can also see a table. There is also an old sofa here,
covered with blankets. The room is poorly lit, but you can see that there is a
lot of garbage thrown around here.
name: Brigands' Secret Hideout

desc:
This is the inner sanctum of the Brigands. This must be the treasure room, since
it is separated from the main room. This room is also dimly lit, making you feel
uncomfortable about this whole place. The only exit is to the west.
name: Hidden Treasure Room

desc:
You are walking up and down on several small hilltops. The landscape around you
is changing. You notice that the country begins to stretch out and to the north
you can see some plains, while south, east and west you can see more hilltops.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
As you travel further out on the flat plains you get more and more annoyed by
the rocks and stones making it a pain to travel here. Now and then you can hear
little sounds as if the rocks and boulders are suddenly coming alive.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
As you make your way out on the flat highlands of the hill you find yourself
obstructed by huge rocks and boulders spread out everywhere. You can see more
rough terrain to the west while you can enter a valley to the north.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
Walking around in this valley seems to be an easy job. The ground is barren and
you can see no bushes or other places giving shelter here. You feel very exposed
up here. In all directions around you, you can see other hilltops except to the
south where you can see some forest.
name: In a Valley

desc:
You are standing in the middle of some small hills stretching both to the north
and to the south. The hills climb and fall endlessly, but you notice that you
slightly move upwards. The surrounding area look rather wasted, cold and dead.
The hills continue to the north and to the south, while the eastern exit is
blocked by huge boulders.
name: Small Hills

desc:
You are walking on some rolling hills. All these small hilltops making you go up
and down all the time nearly make you feel dizzy. The landscape is barren and
seems to be lifeless. The hills continue to the north and to the south.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
You are walking on some rolling hills. You can see much more hills to the north
of you. The landscape around you consists of hilltops, some partly grassy and
some rocky. You can barely see the peaks of some mountains far away to the
north. The wind is coming strongly from the north here, you better move on.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
You have entered a valley stretching east-west from here. The ground is barren,
making it easy to travel upon. You can't see any special path to follow here. To
your northeast you can see a large hilltop, probably the biggest landmark around
here. To the south there seems to be no exit, but maybe you can squeeze your way
through the rocks.
name: In a Valley

desc:
You find yourself in the midst of many small hills. The landcape seems to be the
same in all directions; mostly hills everywhere with some huge boulders and
rocks lying spread among them. To the north you see a rock face, making the way
further north impossible.
name: Small Hills

desc:
You leave the safety of the trail below you and climb up on this huge mountain
plateau. Not much vegetation grows here, but you find a little bit of comfort in
enjoying the great view you have towards the huge range of mountains you can see
to the far north, with its snowy peaks it truly is a majestic view. The hills
continue to the west.
name: Entering the Foothills

desc:
The landscape has indeed changed. All around you, you see boulders, rocks and
stones. Not much grows here, in fact you can see nothing else than rocks. A cool
breath of air blows in your face, refreshing you from these weary travels. You
see some small hills open up in front of you, while to the west you can spot a
clearing.
name: Rough Terrain

desc:
You are standing at the base of a hill. The hill is so steep and prevents you
from further exploration that way. You can spot some patches of grass and
flowers growing here. The only exit is back east.
name: A Clearing

desc:
The landscape has indeed changed. All around you, you see boulders, rocks and
stones. Not much grows here, in fact you can see nothing else than rocks. A cool
breath of air blows in your face, refreshing you from these weary travels. You
have to take it real slow here, twisting your way between large boulders and
stones. The surrounding area looks pretty wasted and dead.
name: Rough Terrain

desc:
You are walking carefully on a stony path. Here you can see stones, boulders and
pebbles of every conceivable size and shape. It is still difficult to walk here.
Far north of here you can see a mountain range, while east and west you see
small hilltops. To the northwest you can see a hilltop. Maybe you get a better
overview over the surrounding area from the top? The path continues to the
north.
name: Stony Path

desc:
You are walking carefully on a stony path. Here you can see stones, boulders and
pebbles of every conceivable size and shape. It is still difficult to walk here.
To the west you can see a little hill, and it looks like there is a little path
even leading up there.
name: Stony Path

desc:
You made your way to the top. From here you can see pretty far away in all
directions. To the north you see some forest and hills, westwards you see a
river, it must be a branch of river Lhun. South of here you can see mostly rocky
terrain and some plains and small clumps of forest. The only exit is back east,
but you notice a huge rock here, slightly loose.
name: Top of a Hill

desc:
The path you try to keep travel along is extremely difficult to walk upon
because of all the rocks and boulders here constantly obstructing your way all
the time. There is a large amount of small pebbles around here, in all sizes and
shapes, but most of them are covered with moss. On your western side there are
small hills, while to the north the plains seem to continue.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The small hills surround you completely. The air here is cold and chilly, and
you get a strange feeling... Or maybe it is only the fresh wind blowing steadily
on your face all the time that annoys you. The landscape seems to change here
and to the north and east you can enter some rocky plains.
name: Small Hills

desc:
You find yourself in the middle of some plains, following no special trail. The
grass that grows here covers your legs. To the south you can hear the sound of
water. The plains seems however to end here and you can spot a little trail to
follow to the north. The plains continues to the south.
name: Plains near the River

desc:
You are standing in some quiet plains. The grass that grow to your waist sways
gently in the wind and a few small bushes grows nearby. As you try to find a
path to follow, you notice there are plains in all directions, save to the south
where the river is slowly making its way southwards.
name: Plains near the River

desc:
You are in some quiet plains. A few small bushes grow among the grass. There is
a big river to the southeast.
name: Plains near the River

desc:
You are standing next to a barn. There's an old well here, probably fully
functionable providing water for the animals around here. There's a river
flowing gently southwards to the east, and to the north you can see some plains.
name: At an Old Well

desc:
When you enter the barn, you notice it is used and inhabitated by animals. The
smell is not that bad. Looking around you see a neatly-kept barn. The walls seem
solid and you don't have to watch where you step. This could be a nice place to
stay over night if you have nowhere else to stay.
name: Inside the Barn

desc:
You are standing in a small clearing. There is a small fireplace here, and small
rocks placed around it to contain it. This would indeed be a nice and peaceful
place to stop by and cook a meal. All around you are some small bushes and the
ground seems to be more fertile here. To the north you can see a barn, while
east runs a river that you just cant reach because the bank. There's a path
leading to the south.
name: Small Clearing

desc:
The vegetation here is thick and lush, of the very deepest green, each blade of
grass long and broad. The field stretches further north, and there seems to be a
small building, possibly a barn, standing to the north-east.
name: Pasture Fields

desc:
When you stand in the middle of this field, you can see that the grass is more
thick and juicy than other places around you. This would definitely be
appetizing to a cow. However - to the north the land grows more wild and you can
see huge boulders and rocks around. The only exit is to the south.
name: Pasture Fields

desc:
Through this hidden entrance you have managed to go inside; a small cavern dimly
lit by some torches hanging on the wall. This must be the very entrance to a
dwarven mine. The air is rather dusty here. You can go further into the cave
complex to the east, or you can go up.
name: Small Cavern

desc:
Further into the cave, the walls have become smoother to the touch of your
hands. Dust seems to be rising from below to rest under your feet. Some metal
tracks run along the floor with an empty mining cart left unattended.
name: Large Hall

desc:
This seems to be a blindway. The tunnel is very narrow and stops suddenly here.
It looks like the work here has been abandoned. Maybe because they did not find
any ore? You see a huge black chasm open up when you look down.
name: Dead End Tunnel

desc:
You find yourself standing in a big room. This must be one of the dozens of
mineshafts probably in this cave complex. You can go further into the caves to
the north, and to the east you see a smaller room.
name: Mine Shaft

desc:
You find yourself standing in a big room. This must be one of the dozens of
mineshafts probably in this cave complex. The air is dusty and not clean at all.
To the east you can see a hollow shaft covered with a plank.
name: Mine Shaft

desc:
This is the storage room for the caves. In here the working tools are organized
neatly and they all seem to be brand new. The metal tracks end here at an
unloading zone. Many carts are sitting on the side collecting dust and small
rocks from the cave walls. The only exit is back west.
name: Storage Room

desc:
You have ended up on a dead end. Below you the water rushes past you. Further
down towards the south the river moves lazily at a steady rate. The water from
up here has indeed a long journey towards the Grey Havens.
name: On a Cliff

desc:
You are walking on a well-prepared trail, making it easy to travel upon. To the
east of you, you can see a river flowing lazily southwards. Far to the west you
can see a forest rising up, but it's still a long distance there. The trail
continues to the north, south and west.
name: Small Trail

desc:
From here you can go north to a trail or enjoy your stay here by the river. The
path you travel upon is broad and wide, making it a pleasure to travel. You just
admire the river and you are appalled by its greatness. It mesmerizes you
whispering about the tales of the old in your ear.
name: Small Trail

desc:
The trail is wide and well-worn, making it a pleasure to walk upon. To both
sides some grass and small trees grow. You can hear the birds singing around
you, and you can hear the bubbeling sound of water not far away. The path
continues to the south and east.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The path is easy to travel upon and you feel comfortable walking here. You feel
tired and only if the site was less friendly than it is here you might dream of
resting. Some strange force regenerates you in some way. At least you think it
is the case. Maybe it is just some kind of magic acting on your mind?
name: Small Trail

desc:
The bridge you are standing on looks strong enough that you can walk over it
with no problems. Its construction seems fairly old, yet the timbers are intact
and show no cracks. Flowing under the bridge is a small brook, a tributary to
the much larger river in the west. From the woods on your east, sounds of
wood-splitting can be heard. The rhythmical beat of axe to wood is in sharp
contrast to the otherwise serene atmosphere.
name: A Bridge over the Tributary

desc:
The small hut visible through the trees to the west is of a plain nature. The
occupant must like his privacy out here, as the dwelling is off the beaten path.
To your north lies a small bridge, passing over a stream that winds east-west.
The sound of a large waterway can be heard in the east. Birds abound in this
area, as do other small animals.
name: Trail to a Dwarven Home

desc:
You are standing here, hesitating to enter the hut. The dwelling is well
crafted, and looks to be of Dwarven origin. It has many windows, thus reducing
your chances for a surprise visit. In the east, you hear the sound of moving
water. Trees flourish in these parts, making shelter for the animals, as well as
an ample food supply. Eastwards the track continues, and the entrance to the hut
is just to your west.
name: Outside a Dwarven Home

desc:
The forest is rather thick here, making you feel a tad uncomfortable. The trees
are growing very close to each other. The thick vegetation here makes it hard to
move on deeper into the forest. The place is shady because only a few sunbeams
reach the soft ground here. To the south you can see some huge pawprints.
name: In the Forest

desc:
As your eyes get used to this dark place, you get a quick view of the cave. It
seems to be a sleeping place of some bears! The stone is naturally made, and the
cave's walls are rough hard stone. The only exit is to the north.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
The forest is very dense here. The sun can barely break through to the soft
ground, and there is no wind blowing at all in here. The sound of breaking
branches under your feet startles you every time you make a step. You can see
the forest in all directions around you.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Here the path makes a sharp turn, still following the riverside. Thick grass
grows on these plains, only broken by the broad path you happen to follow. The
grass is tall and you imagine you see small things moving about in it. To the
north you can enter a river. The footpath seems to continue forever to the east,
but in the distance to the east you can see some light forest. To the west at
the river bank, you can see some piles of logs.
name: Small Trail by the River

desc:
A seemingly endless field starts here, just at the border of a pine forest. You
can see a path winding from the south suddenly end in the grass behind you. To
the north you see the endless plains rolling on and on. To the west and south, a
grove of trees makes it harder to advance in that direction.
name: Plains

desc:
The forest grows thick, making your journey hard and difficult. However, the
pines seem to open themselves to the west, south, and east. To the east you can
even see the vast plains beyond the trees. You are totally surrounded by the
quiet noises of the wilderness.
name: Sparse Pine Forest

desc:
You are standing on the neat and well kept fields, just at the border of a pine
forest to the south. It seems to be too dense to walk through easily. You see a
trail winding to the north and west. The grass, green as it could be, invites
you to pause the journey and take a rest.
name: Small Field by the Trail

desc:
The plains seem to be more friendly here as you discover farmed fields to the
west of you. Someone certainly does a good job taking care of this land. But
still the plains remain equally beautiful. You can turn either south or north,
where the plains seem to extend endlessly.
name: Plains

desc:
These fields seem to be cultivated by a farmer with an extensive knowledge of
the land. Far from the civilization you stare at the sky, dreaming embraced by
the long denied peace. You wonder how long this peace will last when you know
that the evil in the world is restless?
name: Small Field

desc:
You convince yourself more and more of the beauty of this field. What you value
the most is a warm feeling of peace and safety here. Those who live here for
sure are lucky and happy people!
name: Small Field

desc:
You step down into a foul cave. The cave seem to be natural, maybe made for many
years ago. The darkness here is blinding, and the smell of animal is hanging in
the air. On the ground you can see small broken bones and skeletons of some
unlucky animals. You can see the cave opens up to the north of you.
name: Small Cave

desc:
You are inside a small, dark cave. The floor is covered by white bones and small
pieces of meat. The air is warm and thick in here. The walls are made of dirt,
and covered by tree roots.
name: Small Cave

desc:
You wonder what was the purpose of building this narrow ditch. After a long
meditation you discover that it probably served as an irrigating tool. After
making this discovery you decide to move on. The plains extend to the north,
east and south of here.
name: A Narrow Ditch

desc:
Your travels across Arda made you completely aware of its size. To the east you
still see more and more of Arda as the plains stretch endlessly. You see no
sense walking east, so north and west are good choices to take, as you see
something interesting at a distance.
name: Plains

desc:
The field has been farmed with an experienced hand. Whoever lives here, can't
know much about evil since the surroundings seem to be filled with natural
peace. The field seems to expose utter beauty as you admire the fertile soil on
which crops are being grown. It is a dream of many to find themselves a place
similar to this and live their lives happily.
name: Small Field

desc:
Field seems neet and well kept. You are sure that those who live here do not
know much about wars or evil. The trail winds to the west. You reflect on your
adventures as you realise how innocent this place is comparing to those you have
visited in your life.
name: Small Field by the Trail

desc:
You have arrived at a small house. You can smell food here. In the windows of
the hut you see some shadows moving here and there. It must be those to whom the
field to the south belongs.
name: In Front of a Small Hut

desc:
It isn't very rich here, but for sure it is neat and clean. You see that
everything here is in perfect order. All objects are in proper spots. There is
not a better place to rest your bones.
name: A Small Hut

desc:
Plains extend in every possible direction. Some strange tracks on the ground
tell you that it's not as safe as you would judge from the emptiness surrounding
you. To the west there is some kind of farmed field. North, south and east, you
see nothing else but grass.
name: Plains

desc:
Suddenly, you are certain that if you keep on walking to the east, you might die
during your journey. Not because of dangers, but from the thirst. Plains extend
forward from here, no end in sight. You'd better choose to go north or west,
where at least you believe to reach the end someday.
name: Plains

desc:
The plains have no end, that's what cames into your mind when you look east. To
the north, west and south the plains at least seem to have an end. The thick
grass here covers your feets and you make no sound at all when you travel
through here. There is a fresh air blowing in your face as you travel here.
name: Plains

desc:
Plains, that is all you see around you. The wind blows and dances in grass
slowly, soothing. You fill your lungs with fresh air. You can proceed in every
direction to discover more of the plains. As you look down to the ground, you
notice some tracks that any hunter would consider interesting. Perhaps this trip
will not be that boring at all?
name: Plains

desc:
After walking through the empty plains, you find extreme delight in the dense
mass of trees to the north and west, even though they are too dense to allow
your passage. More plains are to the east and south. To the west you see what
seems to be a garden of some kind.
name: Plains

desc:
The beautiful plains reach the line of the land to the east. Far ahead their end
could surely be found after a long tiring journey. Would you take its challenge?
Or is the worn trail to the west a better choice for you?
name: Plains by the Trail

desc:
The plains extend on and on, never stopped by anything until they reach the east
line of the horizon. So tiring and pointless it seems to walk there, that you
prefer going elsewhere. South and west seem to be good choices. There you can
hope to get to the end of the plains soon.
name: Plains

desc:
The plains are vast. They seem neverending. The breath seems to engulf you as
you walk on and on without ending. Maybe these plains lead nowhere? To the west,
you can see a trail that surely leads somewhere. You wonder what is the best
choice to take..
name: Plains by the Trail

desc:
The wind blows hard here high above the plains which spread far and wide toward
the west. West behind the wall, a steep cliff prevents entry of all enemies
without wings. North is the highest point on the hills and in the face of the
rock entry way is hewn leading down into darkness. Many dry bushes grow here
whistling in the wind. This place provides a perfect place to watch everything
that moves upon it. A plaque hangs on the old ruined wall.
name: Old Outpost

desc:
Along the smooth granite walls stand some marble statues, adorned with small
shimmering gemstones. As there are no windows, light is provided by large,
flickering torches which make the gemstones glisten as the light reflects in
them. From the floor to the ceiling the room measures at least forty feet so
that every sound made here echoes several times. In the middle of the room, a
large spiral staircase starts its long, winding way to the next floor of the
tower.
name: Inside Elostirion

desc:
You find yourself at the very end of the forest. The ground here is soft and
pleasant to walk upon. Looking in all directions here, except west gives, you an
impression of the mighty mountainous terrain up here in the Blue Mountains. You
feel the fresh air here blowing gently in your face from the north. The only
exit here is back into the forest to the west.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
You find yourself in the middle of a big clearing. There is an old fireplace
here which shows signs of a recent fire that was burning in it. You can see far
away in all directions here. To the west you can see large boulders and small
hilltops. It must be a pain to walk there. To the east and south you can see a
more low hilltops and even spot a few clumps of trees around. To your immediate
north, there is an old house or inn perhaps.
name: A Big Clearing

desc:
The river runs from north to west. The current is slow, water clear and you can
spot small shiny fishes swimming around. The river makes a sharp turn here to
the west. To the south you can see an excellent shore line for docking. To the
west you can see some high mountain far in the distance, with its white snowy
peaks.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river flows lazily towards the south from the east. It is not especially
deep, but you need a boat to wade across it, something you have no interest in,
since the eastern side of the river is impossible to enter from here, due to
huge rocks standing right at the side of water. You can enter some plains to the
north and west.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river flows lazily south bound. Although this river is not known especially
for it's strong current, it's wide and deep enough to make travel across it
uncomfortable. To the north you can see an old well, while to the west a steep
bank impossible to climb. The river continues its flowing smoothly to the south.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river flows lazily south bound. The cool breeze here keeps you floating and
daydreaming of past adventures. Some light forest covers the eastern bank of the
river, while west you can see small hills in the distance. The river continues
to the north and south, or you can leave up to the sturdy bridge you see above
your head.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river flows lazily here. Although this river is not known especially for
it's strong current, but it's wide and deep enough to make travel across it
uncomfortable. You feel relaxed being here, and you can see some trees to the
north and west creating little shades in the water. The river continues to the
west and north.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river flows lazily south bound. Although this river is not known especially
for it's strong current, but it's wide and deep enough to make travel across it
uncomfortable. You can see a small path following the river at your western
side. The sound of all the birds singing around here, make you feel relaxed and
comfortable.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river makes it way along and you feel a small breeze to the north. The water
is clear, but the currents seem to change suddenly, maybe you could catch some
fish or something. There is definitely something in this river. The river
continues to the south and east.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Here the river make a sharp turn westward. The river is surrounded by bushes and
small trees plunging some of their leaved branches into the calm water. The
river seems to be deeper here, but the clear water here still make it possible
to see the ground clear. You can enter some plains on the southern bank here.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are rafting through a nice calm river. Moving circles in the water with your
hands, you slowly drift into deep thought. Suddenly you notice the river a bit
more brave. On your east side you can see a trail following the river, that
continues to the south.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are at the sandy shore of the large river. The riverbanks are not very steep
here which makes it possible to reach a trail just south of here. North and west
of here you can see the river flowing through the landscape with a steady flow.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are on a large river which slowly makes it's way from the north to the south
through the rather flat and smooth landscape. There is a trail to the west, but
its impossible to climb the steep shore. To the east the the river becomes even
deeper. A bird's song lingers softly in the air.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You find yourself floating along the river. A few old logs lie along the
northern bank. Dark little shifts move through the water. Most likely just some
bass swimming along. The water is rather shallow here, and you can hear the
ripples rock the raft slightly beneath you. Still the river is very wide here
and you would probably not be able to wade across it. To your direct west, you
can see a well-trodden path.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are on a large river which slowly makes its way south through the rather
flat and smooth landscape. There is a small delta of a tributary river to the
west. An old wooden bridge leads across the delta.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are on an island that protrudes from the middle of the river. Joined to
another small land-mass to your east, this little bank is formed of packed dirt,
probably the result of a land shift. It has been here for a while as evidenced
by a few small trees that grow out of the ground. Birds nest here and chirp
softly, but land mammals have a difficult time navigating the swift river
currents.
name: A Small Island in the River Lhun

desc:
You are at the point where the river passes south of a small island. The water
is as clear here as in the rest of the river and as you cast an eye downswards
you see the stone covered bottom slope slightly towards the east where the river
deepens.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are in the middle of the river which continues it's path towards the south.
West of here is a bend in the river where river surrounds a small island.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Here the river narrows somewhat and the current increases a little bit. A small
island rises out of the stream east of you, inviting you to a short rest. To the
south the river continues its way through the landscape of Eriador.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Here the river narrows as it passes east of a small island and as it narrows the
pace of the water increases heavily. The otherwise calm surface has turned into
a surge which completely interrupt any attentions to peer down into the water.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Here the river makes a turn towards the west as it passes a trail which connects
land and water. Three large boulders lies heavily against the bottom in the
shallow water.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The slow moving water surrounds you, except for the southwest where there lies a
small island. West of here the water gets shallower and appears to be barely
moving. The gentle current flows to the south, while the main part of the river
lies nearby to the southeast. The way north is blocked by a steep bank of thick
mud.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Here the river's current is divided by a muddy bar extending to the south. The
main channel continues lazily southward on the east side of the bar. To your
west, a portion of the current flows over an impassible, shallow mud flat and
continues southward on the far side of the bar. The bank north of here is too
steep to climb.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Here a shallow mud bar, extending from the north bank of the river, has become
overgrown with bushes and leafy shrubs. Colorful songbirds sing in the trees by
day; by night, a chorus of deep-throated frogs can be heard from the shallows.
Northwest of this island the river flows over an impassible mud bank, too
treacherous to walk on and too shallow to paddle or swim.
name: An Island in the River

desc:
You are slowly drifting away southwards towards the Grey Havens. As you look
around you notice a small island to your direct west. On your eastern side you
can see a trail slowly following the river northwards towards unknown
destination.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are on the river between two islands, one north and one south of you. The
water is calm in the lee of the northern island; perhaps there is a slight
current from east to west.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are on a narrow island in the River Lhun whose long dimension is north and
south. Lush bushes and trees have grown up on this muddy bar. The island seems
to be visited seldom, if ever, and there are no signs of large animals here.
name: North End of an Island

desc:
The river continues peacfully from the south to the north. This delta area of
the Lhun meeting the Nan Duin makes huge masses of water here. You are
surrounded by water in all direction, and only if you take a good look, you can
see a small trail to your east. The current is not very strong, and there is no
problem for you to stay on the the same spot.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river continues peacfully from the south to the north. The current is not
very strong, and there is no problem for you to stay on the the same spot. To
the west you see a quite large island, covered with trees.
name: River Lhun

desc:
Here the tributary mixes its waters with the main river. Just above your head is
a low bridge which you can climb up to without any difficulty. The tributary
arrives from the west and quickly loses its identity when thrown together with
the larger flow. Fish are mingling among the rocks, looking like they are
hungry.
name: A Small Delta

desc:
You can hear water running through below the plank. Some light from the torches
attached to the wall here sneaks into the darkness below to uncover a deserted
cavern. Further east you can see how another huge cave stretches out. To the
west you can see a mineshaft.
name: Plank

desc:
You have entered a dwarven mine. This mine is in working order and you can spot
metal tracks ending up here. The cave is well-lit by torches attached to the
walls. The only exit is back west.
name: Deep inside the Caves

desc:
This large alcove does not appear to be part of the mine, but instead has been
set up as a workshop. The walls are lined with shelves packed with carefully
labeled boxes, bags, and jars. Several copper and iron cauldrons of various
sizes are lined up along the south wall. Above them is another shelf that bears
a number of tightly sealed ceramic jugs.
name: Dyemaker's Alcove

desc:
This appears to be a place previously used to meet in secret. Lots of bottles
and broken glass lies strewn about. There are scorch marks on the walls where
torches have hung and a blackened circle of stones in the middle of the floor
where countless fires appear to have been lit. Rotten wooden boxes lie in one
corner, any once valuable content most likely taken away or crumbled to dust.
name: Large Round Cavern

desc:
Built into the white granite walls, beautifully arched sleeping alcoves cover
most of the area of the room. Heavy curtains, made out of the finest cotton with
delicate inlays of silver thread, which effectively reduce noise and light hang
in front of every alcove. Placed in-between the alcoves, stands sturdy weapon
racks filled with all kinds of weaponry. In the middle of the room, a large
fireplace is placed, providing the room with light and heat.
name: Guard's Quarters

desc:
The earthen floor and walls are mouldy and damp. In one spot, a small pool has
formed from water coming from the ceiling. The walls are crumbling and thick
roots poke out through the ceiling from above. Some of the walls have small
holes, with pathways of rat droppings between them. A few small bones, belonging
to a small animal, sit in one corner. The tunnel is quite uneven, creating a
bumpy path that ascends from the east to the south.
name: Underground Tunnel

desc:
The precariousness of this location could hardly be overstated, as the walls and
ceiling are barely holding up. Large, spongy chunks of turf and peat are hanging
down from above, impeding one's vision and ability to move. The smell in here is
that of musty, recently uncovered soil, not unlike the first shovel-full taken
from an overgrown mound of compost. Several earth-packed mounds have formed in
this tunnel, the aftermath of a collapse that blocked the tunnel entrance. One
dirt mound is inundated with bones, straw, leather and small bones, forming a
small nest.
name: Collapsed Tunnel

desc:
The steep walls on three sides casts this part of the dry gulch into murky
shadows. The floor of the gulch is mostly made up of sand and small rocks, with
a layer of solid bedrock underneath. Few plants could grow in this unfriendly
environment. To the west lies a small niche, even darker than this area.
name: Shaded Dry Gulch

desc:
It looks like something has managed to crack the wall to the east and carve out
a small rocky hollow here. As time has passed, the area has become a sheltered
refuge for any animals that want shade from the sun. The small cracks lining the
walls are not large enough to be used as foot or hand holds to climb upward, but
could be used by small animals that may want to find some shelter.
name: Small Rocky Niche

desc:
This cave is small and dark. The floor is sandy, and shows tracks of snakes
coming in and out. There are a few skeletons of small animals littering the
floor, and some dry snakeskins. There is a small amount of light filtering in
from the opening to the south.
name: Cool, Dry Cave

desc:
Along the rock walls some barrels and chests are situated, making this cellar
look like some sort of storage. The air is stuffy and murky as the hatch in the
ceiling is so well sealed that no fresh air slips by it. A fragile ladder is the
only connection to the outside world.
name: Storage Cellar

desc:
The snow-white ceiling is lifted to almost thirty feet in height by marble
pillars, inlaid with small sparkling gems. The room is lit by small, carved
lanterns situated every few feet along the thick, granite wall. Beside the
lanterns, small archery slits and large quivers, filled with arrows are
situated. The winding staircase is continuing both up and downward in the tower.
name: Inside Elostirion

desc:
Small, exquisitely carved lamps hangs every few feet around the smooth white
walls. In the west wall a large, arched window show a magnificent view of the
surrounding hills. Around two large oaken tables stands eight robust chairs,
ornamented with delicate inlays on ivory and small glistering gems. On the floor
a couple of large, soft carpets efficiently reduce the echoes in the large
dining hall. The spiral staircase continue both up and down the tower.
name: Dining Hall

desc:
This kitchen is full of jars, glassware, knives, spoons and forks, plates, and
almost every utensil there is. The northern wall is covered with shelves
containing food and fruit from every corner of Arda. In a corner stands some
large barrels filled with wine from the elven kingdom of Lothlorien. In front of
the large iron stove, some white marble has been inlaid in the floor to prevent
sparks from setting the polished oak floor on fire.
name: Kitchen

desc:
A large fireplace is situated in the middle of the room just a few feet to the
left from where the spiral staircase ends. In front of the fireplace, two large
armchairs stand beside a low table. The light from the fireplace is reflected by
small gems, inlaid in the snow-white, arched roof. A large window, facing south,
show a magnificent view of the surrounding lands. A small ladder leads up to the
roof of the tower.
name: Top Chamber of Elostirion

desc:
High marble pillars lift the white, arched roof to an impressive height. The
white granite walls are covered by all kinds of paintings, displaying old and
great days throughout the elven history. Small, gem-inlayed lanterns provide the
room with light as the room is without windows. Broad oak planks constitute the
floor, polished to an almost mirror like shine. In the middle of the room, two
large armchairs and a small table with a chessboard is situated.
name: Inside Elostirion

desc:
In the western wall a large window is situated, showing a magnificent view of
the Tower Hills. The arched, white roof is decorated with delicate inlays of
black marble and small gems which gleams softy. Small torches are placed every
five feet around the walls, sending their flickering light across the room. The
sturdy oak floor is smooth and so polished it almost resembles a mirror.
name: Hall of the Palantir

desc:
The roof is flat with a large chimney in the middle. Around the sides there is a
low wall, apparently built to prevent people from falling off the roof. The view
from up here is amazing: on a clear day one might even catch a glimpse of the
ocean, far, far away to the west. The only safe way down from here is through
the hatch in the oaken roof.
name: Roof of Elostirion

desc:
A sparse forest starts here. To the west you see the trail. To the east and
south the forest becomes darker and unpredictable. Nothing dangerous seems to be
there, but you, an experienced wanderer should know better. Sometimes evil can
take on a form that noone would expect!
name: Sparse Pine Forest

desc:
You are in a light forest. You look above and you see that trees don't obstruct
too much light from entering through the crowns. Because of that a variety of
bushes grows here. Some small animals run around very fast, so fast that you
can't see what they are.
name: Sparse Pine Forest

desc:
You are in the middle of a large river which slowly strives from the north to
the south with a steady flow of water. The surface is somewhat stirred up by
strong currents. Through the clear water you can see small fishes swim beneath
you.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are in the middle of a large river which slowly makes its way from the
northeast to the southwest with a steady flow. A trail reveals itself on the
river bank to the north and a small island can be made out to the south. Far to
the west, a small bridge leaps over a tributary of the river.
name: River Lhun
